Try HARD to REFUTE this lever. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read /home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V2.md, the artifacts at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board, and the grader source if needed.

LEVER: In-prose attribution injection (class F+A: deterministic, sidecar-copied) (L3)
RUBRIC CRITERION CLAIMED: Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized :: synthesizes a broad and representative selection of current, high-quality academic literature (from the specified journal articles) (comprehensiveness w=0.15); also Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles' (instruction-following w=0.15) — since RACE deletes all [n] markers and the bibliography, prose naming is the ONLY source-quality channel that reaches the judge [comprehensiveness + instruction-following]
CHANGE: New deterministic transform: for each FACT sentence, key its [n] to /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank10_sections_compose/bibliography.json (fields: authors, venue, year, doi — measured: 32/105 have venue, 26 doi, authors sparse at top level but present in baskets) and prepend/substitute attribution byte-copied from that record: 'Acemoglu and Restrepo, in the Journal of Economic Perspectives,' — and replace every orphan subject ('The study', 'One analysis', 'That study') with the named referent of its own [n]. Metadata backfill pass first: Crossref lookup by DOI/URL; only strings returned by Crossref for that exact DOI may ship. A validator asserts every injected name/venue is a byte-substring of the bib/Crossref record keyed by that sentence's citation — fabrication is structurally impossible.
CLAIMED: +0.015 to +0.020 overall (this is the measured mechanism by which cellcog's words buy comprehensiveness: author + venue + N per claim)
ADMITTED FAILURE MODE: Coverage: if backfill only resolves ~50 of 105 entries, half our facts stay orphaned; worse, naming the VENUE of a blog source (amplifai.com) would advertise the violation — so attribution injection must run AFTER L5's source filter and only on journal-tier entries. Template monotony ('X, in Y, found...' 60 times) reads as machine output — need 4-5 rotation templates
FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: REVISED CONTRACT — from hedged gloss to contained adjudication. The old contract's guarantee ('worst case is a hedged, uncited, capped, contradiction-screened relational gloss') aimed at the wrong genre: the rubric pays for ADJUDICATION ('areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty... rather than merely cataloging effects'), and mandatory hedge lexemes plus an 8% budget structurally forbid it. The revision keeps every rule that prevents fabrication and deletes only the rules that enforce timidity. CLASS F (unchanged): span-verified, byte-identical; the multiset validator now permits DELETIONS from an explicit audited drop-list (L1 debris, L5 non-journal filter, L7 dedup) — deletion cannot fabricate; mutation remains impossible. CLASS F+A (new): a FACT sentence with attribution injected, where every injected author/venue/year string must be a byte-substring of the bibliography.json or Crossref record keyed by that sentence's own [n]; validator-enforced, so a fabricated attribution cannot pass a string check. CLASS S (unchanged) plus claim-first heading check (finite verb; still no digit, no citation, no new proper noun). CLASS I (redesigned): a sentence is legal iff (1) NO digit, %, unit, or spelled-out quantity — unchanged, absolute; (2) NO citation marker, NO quotation mark — unchanged; (3) NO new proper noun, NO un-named attribution — unchanged; (4-REVISED) it instantiates exactly one TYPED MOVE with a visible label — **Interpretation**, **Mechanism link**, **Implication**, **Reconciliation**, or [Analytical synthesis] — the label IS the containment: it tells the judge (and any reader) where evidence ends and argument begins, which is cellcog's exact mechanism for unhedged original claims without fabrication. Hedge lexemes are permitted, never required. Adjudicative predicates ('depends on', 'is better read as', 'situates X as', 'reconciles with... if') are legal. Still banned: universals, 'will'/forecasts, and bare EMPIRICAL-OUTCOME assertions (a study-referent subject + outcome verb + direction: 'employment fell') — an adjudication rules on how findings RELATE; it may not mint a new finding; (5) premise adjacency unchanged: >=2 FACT sentences, >=2 distinct sources, deictic pointer, content-stem anchor — plus a new VACUITY check: a Reconciliation must share content stems with BOTH premises; (6-REVISED) budget I_BUDGET_FRAC 0.08 -> 0.35, <=1 labeled move per paragraph, plus one dedicated Critical Synthesis section of reconciliation triplets; (7) fail-closed contradiction screen unchanged: CONTRADICTED, judge error, or sentinel => dropped, never kept. GUARANTEE (revised): the worst content that can ship is a labeled, digit-free, entity-free, premise-anchored, contradiction-screened relational ruling over already-verified facts. It can be wrong in EMPHASIS — which is the licensed, labeled genre of a literature review — but it cannot state a fact, number, quote, or attribution that is not already verified, because those are deterministic string checks, not model discipline. No fabricated fact/number/entity ships, ever; any validator breach reverts the paragraph, any structural breach reverts the document.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
JUDGE-INVISIBILITY: RACE strips citations and the bibliography, and scores our report SIDE BY SIDE against a 0.5102 reference in ONE call. Would the judge actually SEE and REWARD this — and would it move a criterion from the "6-8 Good" band into "8-10 Excellent"? There is NO credit for being a better 7.

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

2	# (Every number here is MEASURED from primary artifacts on disk or read from the grader's own source code.)
3	
4	## 1. THE BOARD (task 72, OUR evaluator = GPT-5.5, per-task scores from the benchmark's own raw_results)
5	| system | OVERALL | compreh | INSIGHT | instr-follow | readability | words | H3 | med-para | bullets |
6	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
7	| **bodhi** (task-72 winner) | **0.5441** | 0.5441 | 0.5457 | 0.5559 | 0.5199 | **4,361** | 40 | 59w | 14 |
8	| lunon | 0.5406 | 0.5437 | 0.5594 | 0.5323 | 0.5037 | 11,476 | 50 | 71w | 11 |
9	| WhaleCloud | 0.5396 | 0.5417 | 0.5429 | 0.5393 | 0.5284 | 7,892 | 33 | 63w | 26 |
10	| dalpha | 0.5252 | 0.5213 | 0.5200 | 0.5350 | 0.5284 | 4,039 | 0 | 38w | 0 |
11	| sourcery | 0.5205 | 0.5211 | 0.5397 | 0.5033 | 0.5035 | 9,849 | 38 | 66w | 106 |
12	| **gemini-2.5-pro-DR = THE REFERENCE** | 0.5102 | 0.5111 | 0.5035 | 0.5187 | 0.5100 | 7,907 | 0 | 131w | 61 |
13	| openai-DR | 0.4743 | 0.4789 | 0.4475 | 0.4982 | 0.4859 | 8,513 | 0 | 170w | 13 |
14	| **POLARIS (rank10)** | **0.4382** | 0.4549 | 0.4238 | 0.4409 | **0.3774** | 7,742 | **0** | **677w** | 0 |
15	| grok | 0.4316 | 0.4292 | 0.3873 | 0.4670 | 0.4706 | 1,060 | 0 | 72w | 0 |
16	| perplexity | 0.4241 | 0.4228 | 0.4015 | 0.4391 | 0.4507 | 1,510 | 0 | 58w | 0 |
17	(cellcog-max = 0.5578 corpus-wide #1; it publishes no per-task breakdown. Its task-72 artifact: 16,334w, 31 H3, 75w paras, 0 bullets.)
18	**"ADORE 52.65" IS FICTION** — absent from the backing data of both boards. Do not plan against it.
19	
20	## 2. THE GAP, DECOMPOSED (us -> bodhi)
21	| dim | gap | weight | overall |
22	|---|---|---|---|
23	| INSIGHT | +0.122 | 0.32 | **+0.039** |
24	| instruction-following | +0.115 | 0.25 | +0.029 |
25	| comprehensiveness | +0.089 | 0.29 | +0.026 |
26	| readability | **+0.143** | 0.14 | +0.020 |
27	| | | | **= +0.114 -> 0.552** |
28	**OUR READABILITY (0.3774) IS THE WORST SCORE ON THE ENTIRE BOARD** — below grok and perplexity, the two systems we beat.
29	
30	## 3. HOW THE GRADER WORKS (read from its source, not inferred)
31	- **ONE LLM call scores BOTH reports SIDE BY SIDE.** Ours = article_1, reference = article_2, always. (race.py:99-104)
32	- **0-10 per criterion**, ~25 criteria. Bands: 4-6 "Average", **6-8 "Good"**, **8-10 "Excellent"**.
33	  We are being read at ~6.6; the reference reads ~9.0. **To beat parity a criterion must cross from "Good" into "Excellent". There is no credit for being a better 7.**
34	- **Overall = ours/(ours+reference)** — COMPRESSIVE. +1.0 raw point on EVERY criterion = only **+0.036 overall**.
35	- **THE JUDGE WRITES A COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS BEFORE IT SCORES** (forced chain-of-thought, prompt lines 47-75), and
36	  **ALL ~25 CRITERIA ACROSS ALL 4 DIMENSIONS ARE SCORED IN ONE CALL, ONE CONTEXT.** Nothing firewalls the dimensions.
37	  **=> CROSS-DIMENSION BLEED IS REAL AND UNINSTRUCTED.** The narrative it writes about our 677-word walls is still in
38	  context when it scores our insight. Our readability disaster is taxing the other 86% of the score.
39	- **Dimension weights (100-task mean): insight 0.352 | comprehensiveness 0.292 | instruction-following 0.215 | readability 0.141.**
40	  Insight + comprehensiveness = **64% of the score**.
41	- **LENGTH IS NEVER MENTIONED IN THE SCORING PROMPT.** No criterion rewards it. Padding is penalised at only ~0.7% of
42	  overall, while substance-bearing expansion pays into 64%. => "longer but DENSER" wins; "longer and thinner" is pointless.
43	- **EXECUTIVE SUMMARIES ARE NOT REWARDED.** Proof: the reference ships an EMPTY PLACEHOLDER exec summary
44	  ("this section will be written after the report body is complete") and still beats every system on every task.
45	- **HEDGING IS NOT REWARDED.** No criterion anywhere pays for softness or uncertainty-as-stance. What IS rewarded (51
46	  criteria, inside the 64%-weight dimensions) is **CRITICAL SCRUTINY**: "Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of
47	  Competing Theories", "Critical Scrutiny of Limitations". **The judge does not want us to sound uncertain. It wants us to ADJUDICATE.**
48	- INSIGHT rubric, verbatim: "deep analysis and original insights rather than simply repeating known information";
49	  "clear logical reasoning and effectively explains causal relationships behind phenomena"; "identifies key issues ...
50	  provides insightful solutions"; "forward-looking thinking, can anticipate trends".
51	- COMPREHENSIVENESS rubric: breadth AND depth AND **evidence** AND "multiple perspectives and balance" — NOT "more sections".
52	- Structure: "Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow" is the largest readability criterion and explicitly names
53	  "distinct heading levels" / "effective headings and subheadings". Tables/charts: max ~2.8% of overall. Worth having, not worth optimising hard.
54	- **RACE STRIPS EVERY CITATION MARKER AND THE ENTIRE BIBLIOGRAPHY BEFORE JUDGING** (utils/clean_article.py, an LLM
55	  cleaner). VERIFIED: our 9,300 words -> 7,692 read; 345 [n] markers -> 0; 105-entry bibliography -> DELETED.
56	  **=> ALL bibliography/citation-quality work scores ZERO. Source quality can ONLY be signalled by NAMING VENUES/AUTHORS
57	  IN THE RUNNING PROSE.** (The 0.5102 reference ships ZERO inline citations.)
58	
59	## 4. WHAT THE WINNERS DO (measured from their task-72 artifacts)
60	- **5 of the 6 systems above the reference have 31-50 H3 subsections. Every system at/below the reference has ZERO.**
61	  (dalpha is the lone exception: 0 H3, but 38-word paragraphs.) **We have 0 H3 and 677-word paragraphs.**
62	- Winners' median paragraph: **59-75 words**. Reference: 131. OpenAI: 170. **Us: 677 — a different regime entirely.**
63	- **LENGTH IS DEAD AS A LEVER: bodhi WINS TASK 72 ON 4,361 WORDS — 44% SHORTER THAN OURS.** dalpha scores 0.5252 on 4,039.
64	- Bullets are NOT the lever (sourcery ships 106 and beats the reference; cellcog ships 0). Tables are NOT the lever (0-5).
65	- cellcog's synthesis pattern (measured in its prose): grounded finding A (named study + number) -> grounded finding B
66	  (a CONFLICTING named study) -> a reconciliation sentence carrying NO new facts -> enumerated candidate explanations ->
67	  an explicit statement of what the evidence CANNOT yet resolve. Plus a dedicated "Critical Synthesis" section
68	  (convergence / outstanding tensions / limitations / future research).
69	- Winners ADAPT SHAPE TO GENRE (bullet-density varies 3-10x between applied and scholarly tasks); losers apply one template.
70	
71	## 5. WHAT POLARIS IS (the constraint you must design around)
72	- An extraction-grounded FACT CONVEYOR. `verify_sentence_provenance` (provenance_generator.py:2098) admits a sentence
73	  only if `len(failures)==0`; the entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that
74	  "introduces a fact, entity, **MECHANISM** ... NOT present in the SPAN".
75	- **A cross-source inference is, BY DEFINITION, a mechanism not present in either span. Our own gate deletes exactly the
76	  sentence type that earns INSIGHT (0.32 — the heaviest weight, and our worst dimension).**
77	- Boundary, precisely: a CONJUNCTION of two sources ("X found 2.1% [a] while Y found 0.4% [b]") PASSES. Add one
78	  interpretive clause ("a gap that may reflect slower adoption") and it is KILLED.
79	- **FAITHFULNESS IS THE MOAT AND IS NOT FOR SALE.** No fabricated fact, number, quote or attribution may ever ship. Any
80	  design that can leak one is unshippable, whatever it scores. We have a working deterministic contract
81	  (scripts/reflow_report.py): FACT (span-verified, byte-identical) / STRUCTURE (assembled from sidecar data) /
82	  INTERPRETATION (no digits, no new proper nouns, no citations, must sit beside >=2 facts from >=2 sources,
83	  contradiction-screened fail-closed). **NOTE: it was built around HEDGING — and the rubric does not reward hedging.
84	  It rewards ADJUDICATION. That contract needs rethinking, not just retuning.**
85	
86	## 6. MEASUREMENT (fixed tonight — use it, never score n=1 again)
87	- Judge noise: **SD = 0.0074** (byte-identical artifact scored 6x). Baseline is tighter (SD 0.0020).
88	- k=5 paired => SE(diff) = 0.0047 => **smallest resolvable effect = +0.0094 at 2 sigma.**
89	- Generation noise (re-composing the same config) is LARGER (~0.016) and applies ONLY to arms needing a new compose.
90	  A report->report transform of a BANKED artifact has ZERO generation noise — cheap AND sensitive. Prefer it.
91	- Our own history: baseline 0.4062 -> rank10 0.4382 = **+0.032, z=10** (real). The depth levers DID work, modestly.
92	  But every subsequent lever (padding removal, source firewall) scored FLAT. And ALL of it was aimed at proxies.
93	
94	## 7. YOUR TASK
95	Design the plan that takes POLARIS from **0.4382** to **the top of this board (0.54-0.56)**.
96	- Ground every lever in the rubric text or a measured artifact. NO PROXIES — we lost a night to them.
97	- State, for each lever: the criterion it moves, the expected points, how it FAILS, and the cheap test that proves the
98	  mechanism fired BEFORE we pay for a 65-minute compose.
99	- Respect the faithfulness moat absolutely. If a lever needs ungrounded prose, say so explicitly and justify the exact
100	  containment.
101	- Be honest about the ceiling. If your plan does not reach 0.54, SAY SO and name the residual.
102	- Where you are UNCERTAIN, SEARCH (papers, GitHub, the artifacts on disk at
103	  /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/). Do not guess.

1	import json
2	import os
3	import time
4	import threading
5	import concurrent.futures
6	from tqdm import tqdm
7	from prompt.clean_prompt import clean_article_prompt_zh, clean_article_prompt_en
8	import logging
9	
10	# Configure logging
11	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')
12	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
13	
14	# Shared LLM-call concurrency cap. All cleaning calls (any chunk of any article)
15	# acquire from this semaphore so total inflight LLM requests ≤ LLM_CONCURRENCY.
16	# Override via env LLM_CONCURRENCY=N. Default 20.
17	_LLM_CONCURRENCY = int(os.environ.get("LLM_CONCURRENCY", "20"))
18	_llm_semaphore = threading.Semaphore(_LLM_CONCURRENCY)
19	logger.info(f"LLM call concurrency cap = {_LLM_CONCURRENCY}")
20	
21	
22	class ArticleCleaner:
23	    def __init__(self, clean_agent):
24	        self.clean_agent = clean_agent
25	        self.min_valid_length = 100
26	    
27	    def _get_clean_prompt(self, language="zh"):
28	        """Return language-specific cleaning prompt template"""
29	        return clean_article_prompt_zh if language == "zh" else clean_article_prompt_en
30	    
31	    def _is_valid_result(self, text):
32	        """Check if cleaning result is valid"""
33	        return text and len(text.strip()) >= self.min_valid_length
34	    
35	    def _update_progress(self, pbar, pbar_lock):
36	        """Update progress bar safely"""
37	        if pbar and pbar_lock:
38	            with pbar_lock:
39	                pbar.update(1)
40	    
41	    def _is_token_limit_error(self, error):
42	        """Check if error is related to token limit"""
43	        error_str = str(error).lower()
44	        return "tokens" in error_str and "less than" in error_str
45	    
46	    _TRUNCATED = "__TRUNCATED__"
47	
48	    def _clean_text(self, text, language="zh", max_retries=None):
49	        """Try to clean text. Returns string (incl. "") on success,
50	        self._TRUNCATED on output overflow, or None after all retries.
51	        max_retries kept for signature compatibility but the canonical budget
52	        is _RETRY_BACKOFFS_S (3 attempts with exponential backoff).
53	        """
54	        clean_prompt = self._get_clean_prompt(language)
55	        user_prompt = clean_prompt.format(article=text)
56	        backoffs = self._RETRY_BACKOFFS_S
57	
58	        last_err = None
59	        for attempt, sleep_s in enumerate([0, *backoffs[:-1]]):
60	            if sleep_s:
61	                time.sleep(sleep_s)
62	            try:
63	                with _llm_semaphore:
64	                    result, stop_reason = self.clean_agent.generate(
65	                        user_prompt=user_prompt, system_prompt="",
66	                        return_metadata=True, stage="clean",
67	                    )
68	                if stop_reason in ("max_tokens", "length"):
69	                    logger.warning(f"Output truncated (stop_reason={stop_reason}), needs chunking")
70	                    return self._TRUNCATED
71	                # Accept any string (including "") — empty means the chunk
72	                # was entirely a reference list, per prompt contract.
73	                if result is not None:
74	                    return result
75	                logger.warning(f"Got None from API, attempt {attempt+1}/{len(backoffs)}")
76	            except Exception as e:
77	                last_err = e
78	                logger.error(f"API call error (attempt {attempt+1}/{len(backoffs)}): {e}")
79	                if self._is_token_limit_error(e):
80	                    logger.info("Article too long, needs chunking")
81	                    return self._TRUNCATED
82	
83	        logger.error(f"Cleaning failed after {len(backoffs)} attempts; last error: {last_err}")
84	        return None
85	        
86	    @staticmethod
87	    def _split_at_boundary(text, target_pos):
88	        """Find nearest sentence boundary to target_pos."""
89	        search_start = max(0, target_pos - 200)

95	    # Pre-chunking thresholds (tokens). One chunk per 50k tokens; <=50k stays whole.
96	    # 50k leaves headroom under GPT-5.5's output cap for reasoning + cleaned text.
97	    _CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP = 50_000
98	    _SPLIT_SEARCH_RADIUS = 5000

100	    # Per-call retry budget when API throws (excluding fatal errors that signal
101	    # truncation or token limit, which short-circuit to recursive chunking).
102	    _RETRY_BACKOFFS_S = (2, 5, 15)

104	    @staticmethod
105	    def _estimate_tokens(text, language):
106	        """Cheap upper-bound token estimate.
107	        zh: 1 char ≈ 1 token (slight over-est, safer).
108	        en: 1 token ≈ 3.5 chars.
109	        """
110	        if not text:
111	            return 0
112	        if language == "zh":
113	            return len(text)
114	        return int(len(text) / 3.5) + 1

116	    @classmethod
117	    def _compute_num_chunks(cls, num_tokens):
118	        """Boundaries at 100k, 200k, 300k, ...
119	        <=100k → 1, (100k,200k] → 2, (200k,300k] → 3, ...
120	        """
121	        if num_tokens <= cls._CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP:
122	            return 1
123	        # ceil(num_tokens / step)
124	        return (num_tokens + cls._CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP - 1) // cls._CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP
125	
126	    @classmethod
127	    def _find_double_newline_split(cls, text, target_pos, radius=None):
128	        """Find split position nearest to target_pos.
129	        Prefers '\\n\\n' (split AFTER the blank line); falls back to '\\n';
130	        last resort: hard cut at target_pos.
131	        """
132	        if radius is None:
133	            radius = cls._SPLIT_SEARCH_RADIUS
134	        n = len(text)
135	        lo = max(0, target_pos - radius)
136	        hi = min(n, target_pos + radius)
137	
138	        best_pos, best_dist = None, None
139	        scan = lo
140	        while scan < hi:
141	            idx = text.find("\n\n", scan, hi)
142	            if idx == -1:
143	                break
144	            dist = abs(idx - target_pos)
145	            if best_dist is None or dist < best_dist:
146	                best_dist, best_pos = dist, idx + 2
147	            scan = idx + 1
148	        if best_pos is not None:
149	            return best_pos
150	
151	        # Fallback: nearest single '\n'
152	        best_pos, best_dist = None, None
153	        for i in range(lo, hi):
154	            if text[i] == "\n":
155	                dist = abs(i - target_pos)
156	                if best_dist is None or dist < best_dist:
157	                    best_dist, best_pos = dist, i + 1
158	        if best_pos is not None:
159	            return best_pos
160	
161	        return target_pos

163	    @classmethod
164	    def _split_into_chunks(cls, text, num_chunks):
165	        """Split text into num_chunks pieces at \\n\\n boundaries near i/N positions."""
166	        if num_chunks <= 1 or not text:
167	            return [text]
168	        n = len(text)
169	        cuts = []
170	        for i in range(1, num_chunks):
171	            target = (n * i) // num_chunks
172	            cuts.append(cls._find_double_newline_split(text, target))
173	        # Ensure cuts are strictly increasing (paranoid: search radii could overlap)
174	        cuts = sorted(set(cuts))
175	
176	        chunks, prev = [], 0
177	        for c in cuts:
178	            if c > prev:
179	                chunks.append(text[prev:c])
180	                prev = c
181	        chunks.append(text[prev:])
182	        return [c for c in chunks if c]

184	    def _clean_one_chunk(self, idx, total, chunk, language, max_retries):
185	        """Clean a single pre-split chunk. Returns text or None on failure.
186	        LLM-call concurrency is gated by the module-level _llm_semaphore inside _clean_text.
187	        """

200	    def _clean_chunked(self, article, num_chunks, language, max_retries):
201	        """Pre-split into num_chunks pieces, clean each in parallel, concatenate.
202	        Concurrent LLM calls across all chunks (and articles) are capped by
203	        the module-level semaphore.
204	        """
205	        chunks = self._split_into_chunks(article, num_chunks)
206	        logger.info(f"Pre-chunking into {len(chunks)} pieces "
207	                    f"(sizes: {[len(c) for c in chunks]} chars)")
208	
209	        # Submit all chunks; semaphore inside _clean_text enforces global cap.
210	        results = [None] * len(chunks)
211	        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(chunks)) as ex:
212	            future_to_idx = {
213	                ex.submit(self._clean_one_chunk, i, len(chunks), c, language, max_retries): i
214	                for i, c in enumerate(chunks)
215	            }
216	            for fut in concurrent.futures.as_completed(future_to_idx):
217	                i = future_to_idx[fut]
218	                results[i] = fut.result()

239	            if result == self._TRUNCATED:
240	                logger.info(f"depth={_depth} half={i} truncated, splitting further")
241	                result = self.chunk_clean_article(half, language, max_depth, _depth + 1)
242	            if result is None:

244	                return None
245	            cleaned_parts.append(result)

import logging

def calculate_weighted_scores(llm_output_json, criteria_data, language="en"):
    """
    Calculates weighted scores based on LLM output and criteria weights.
    
    Args:
        llm_output_json: JSON output from LLM with scoring data
        criteria_data: Criteria configuration with dimension weights
        language: Language of the evaluation (default: "en")
        
    Returns:
        Dictionary with weighted scores for target and reference models
    """
    results = {
        "target": {"dims": {}, "total": 0.0},
        "reference": {"dims": {}, "total": 0.0}
    }
    total_target_score = 0.0
    total_reference_score = 0.0
    
    # Get dimension weights
    dimension_weights = criteria_data.get("dimension_weight", {})
    task_id = criteria_data.get("id", "Unknown") 
    
    # Check if criterions exist
    if "criterions" not in criteria_data or not criteria_data["criterions"]:
        error_msg = f"ID: {task_id} - Missing required criterions data, cannot calculate weighted scores"
        logging.error(error_msg)
        raise ValueError(error_msg)
    
    # Create a mapping from criterion text to weight for easier lookup
    criterion_weights = {}
    for dim, criterions in criteria_data.get("criterions", {}).items():
        criterion_weights[dim] = {crit['criterion']: crit['weight'] for crit in criterions}

    # Record all unmatched criteria for warnings
    unmatched_criteria = set()
    
    for dim, scores_list in llm_output_json.items():
        if not isinstance(scores_list, list):
            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' in LLM output is not a list. Skipping.")
            continue

        if dim not in dimension_weights:
            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' from LLM output not found in criteria dimension weights. Skipping dimension.")
            continue
            
        if dim not in criterion_weights:
            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' from LLM output not found in criteria details. Skipping dimension.")
            continue

        dim_target_weighted_sum = 0.0
        dim_reference_weighted_sum = 0.0
        dim_total_weight = 0.0

        dim_criteria_map = criterion_weights.get(dim, {})
        
        # Skip dimension if no criteria mapping exists
        if not dim_criteria_map:
            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - No criteria mapping found for dimension '{dim}'. Skipping dimension.")
            continue

        for score_item in scores_list:
            if not isinstance(score_item, dict):
                logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Item in scores_list for dimension '{dim}' is not a dictionary. Skipping item: {score_item}")
                continue

            criterion_text_raw = score_item.get("criterion")
            criterion_text = criterion_text_raw.strip() if isinstance(criterion_text_raw, str) else None

            # Check different score field formats
            # 1. Comparative scoring mode: article_1_score and article_2_score
            # 2. Single scoring mode: target_score
            article_1_score_raw = score_item.get("article_1_score")
            article_2_score_raw = score_item.get("article_2_score")
            target_score_raw = score_item.get("target_score")  # Single scoring mode

            # If target_score exists but article_1_score doesn't, assign target_score to article_1_score
            if target_score_raw is not None and article_1_score_raw is None:
                article_1_score_raw = target_score_raw
                # In single scoring mode, article_2_score is not needed

            try:
                article_1_score = float(article_1_score_raw) if article_1_score_raw is not None else None
                article_2_score = float(article_2_score_raw) if article_2_score_raw is not None else None
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Invalid score format for criterion '{criterion_text}' in dimension '{dim}'. Scores: art1='{article_1_score_raw}', art2='{article_2_score_raw}'. Skipping criterion.")
                continue

            # If criterion_text exists and article_1_score is not None
            if criterion_text and article_1_score is not None:
                # Check for exact match
                weight = dim_criteria_map.get(criterion_text)
                
                # If exact match not found, try fuzzy matching
                if weight is None:
                    # First try case-insensitive matching
                    criterion_lower = criterion_text.lower()
                    for key, val in dim_criteria_map.items():
                        if key.lower() == criterion_lower:
                            weight = val
                            break
                    
                    # If still not found, try substring matching
                    if weight is None:
                        for key, val in dim_criteria_map.items():
                            # Check if criterion text contains criteria or criteria contains criterion text
                            if criterion_lower in key.lower() or key.lower() in criterion_lower:
                                weight = val
                                break
                
                # If still no match found, record and use average weight
                if weight is None:
                    unmatched_criteria.add(f"{dim}:{criterion_text}")
                    # Calculate average weight for this dimension's criteria
                    weight = sum(dim_criteria_map.values()) / len(dim_criteria_map)
                    
                dim_target_weighted_sum += article_1_score * weight
                dim_total_weight += weight
                
                # Only calculate reference part if article_2_score exists
                if article_2_score is not None:
                    dim_reference_weighted_sum += article_2_score * weight
            else:
                if criterion_text:
                    if dim not in getattr(calculate_weighted_scores, '_warned_dims', set()):
                        logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Criterion text mismatch for dimension '{dim}': '{criterion_text}'. Available criteria keys start with: {list(dim_criteria_map.keys())[:1] if dim_criteria_map else []}... Check prompt/output/criteria file consistency. Further mismatches in this dim won't be logged fully.")
                        if not hasattr(calculate_weighted_scores, '_warned_dims'): calculate_weighted_scores._warned_dims = set()
                        calculate_weighted_scores._warned_dims.add(dim)
                    else:
                        logging.debug(f"ID: {task_id} - Another criterion mismatch for dimension '{dim}': '{criterion_text}'")
                elif not criterion_text:
                    logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Missing 'criterion' key in score item for dimension '{dim}': {score_item}. Skipping item.")

        if dim_total_weight > 0:
            dim_target_avg = dim_target_weighted_sum / dim_total_weight
            # Only calculate reference average if reference scores exist
            dim_reference_avg = dim_reference_weighted_sum / dim_total_weight if article_2_score is not None else 0
        else:
            dim_target_avg = 0
            dim_reference_avg = 0
            if len(scores_list) > 0:
                logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - No valid criteria scored for dimension '{dim}' despite {len(scores_list)} items in LLM output. Check for systematic mismatches. Dimension average set to 0.")

        results["target"]["dims"][f"{dim}_weighted_avg"] = dim_target_avg
        results["reference"]["dims"][f"{dim}_weighted_avg"] = dim_reference_avg

        dim_weight = dimension_weights.get(dim, 0)
        total_target_score += dim_target_avg * dim_weight
        total_reference_score += dim_reference_avg * dim_weight

    # Log warning if there are unmatched criteria
    if unmatched_criteria:
        logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - {len(unmatched_criteria)} criteria without exact matches: {unmatched_criteria}")
    
    results["target"]["total"] = total_target_score
    results["reference"]["total"] = total_reference_score

    return results

content.text: Three independent lines of attack, all confirmed against primary artifacts. Let me record the verdict.

input.reasoning:
REFUTED on three independent, sufficient grounds. Sources: /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl (task-72 criteria + weights), utils/score_calculator.py, prompt/clean_prompt.py, /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/*.jsonl (task-72 artifacts), /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank10_sections_compose/bibliography.json.

=== 1. ARITHMETIC: the lever cannot pay what it claims, by construction ===
The two named criteria carry w=0.15 INSIDE their dimensions (verified in criteria.jsonl; each dimension's criterion weights sum to 1.0). Overall exposure = 0.15*0.29 (compreh) + 0.15*0.25 (instr-follow) = 0.081 of the score. Overall is compressive: ours/(ours+ref) per dimension (score_calculator.py + brief 3).
Modelling ref_raw=8.0 and back-solving our raw from the board (compreh 0.4549 -> ours 6.68; IF 0.4409 -> ours 6.31):
  +1.0 raw on BOTH target criteria -> +0.0031 overall
  +2.0 raw on BOTH                 -> +0.0061 overall
  +3.0 raw on BOTH (a full Good->Excellent jump on each) -> +0.0090 overall
The 2-sigma resolvable effect at k=5 is +0.0094 (brief 6). So even the ABSURDLY generous case — a 3-point Good-to-Excellent crossing on both criteria simultaneously — lands BELOW the detection floor and at less than HALF the claimed +0.015 to +0.020. The claimed magnitude is unreachable no matter how well the mechanism works. The lever's own framing ("There is NO credit for being a better 7") is the argument against it: crossing into Excellent on these two criteria is worth +0.009, and it is the only thing this lever touches.

=== 2. EMPIRICAL: on the actual board, in-prose attribution does not predict either criterion ===
Measured per-system on the task-72 artifacts (journal-venue mentions + "Name (Year)" + "et al."):
  bodhi        1 journal name,  0 Name(Year), 12 et al. | 4,361w | compreh .5441 (BEST) IF .5559 (BEST) -> WINS TASK 72
  gemini (REF) 5 journal names, 0 Name(Year),  0 et al. | 7,907w | compreh .5111 IF .5187 -> the 0.5102 reference
  lunon       31,  0 Name(Year) | compreh .5437 IF .5323
  WhaleCloud  46, 43 Name(Year) (densest attributor) | compreh .5417 IF .5393 -> BELOW bodhi on both
  openai-DR   50,  6 Name(Year) | compreh .4789 IF .4982 -> BELOW the reference
Among the 7 long-form systems: r(attribution, comprehensiveness) = -0.07, r(attribution, instruction-following) = -0.05. (The apparent +0.37/+0.41 across all 9 is entirely an artifact of grok and perplexity, 1,060w and 1,510w stubs that score badly for length/depth reasons and happen to have zero attributions.) The task-72 WINNER takes the top score on BOTH target criteria while naming ONE journal in the entire report.
The lever's stated evidence ("the measured mechanism by which cellcog's words buy comprehensiveness") is not measurable: cellcog publishes NO per-task breakdown (brief 1). It is confounded with cellcog's 16,334 words and 31 H3s.
What the two systems that beat us ACTUALLY do for source quality: (a) gemini-ref uses AGGREGATE characterization — "A strong consensus emerges from the literature...", "A dominant theme in the literature is...", "remains contested", "Influential studies, such as the one by Frey and Osborne", "prominently associated with economists like Daron Acemoglu and David Autor" — plus a "Dominant Theoretical Frameworks and Empirical Approaches" section; (b) bodhi uses METHOD — "IV designs", "event-study designs using exogenous shocks", "(This evidence is correlational rather than a clean causal estimate)". Neither does venue-per-claim. The channel that reaches the judge is METHOD and ADJUDICATION, not the venue string.

=== 3. FAITHFULNESS: FATAL. The validator's safety guarantee is FALSE ===
The lever asserts "every injected name/venue is a byte-substring of the bib/Crossref record keyed by that sentence's citation — fabrication is structurally impossible." I audited bibliography.json (105 entries). The `venue` field is PARSE DEBRIS, not metadata:
  - 73/105 have no venue; only 4/105 have top-level authors; 26/105 have a DOI.
  - Of the 32 with a venue, 18 are explicitly NON-journal: NBER Working Paper x5, OECD x3, BLS x3, arXiv x2, IMF x2, SSRN, Congressional Research Service, World Economic Forum.
  - Of the 14 "journal-ish" ones, at least 6 are FALSE or invented:
      [46] venue "Review of AI"  <- scraped out of its own TITLE ("A Systematic Review of AI's Impact on Employment...")
      [47] venue "Review of Skill Transformation in the Industry" <- scraped out of its own title
      [100] venue "Review of Underlying Concepts and Evidence" <- scraped out of its own title
      [41] venue "American Economic Review" on an arXiv preprint (doi 10.48550/arXiv.2303.10130)
      [82] venue "American Economic Review" on "Research on AI and the labor market... - The Hamilton Project" — a BROOKINGS BLOG POST carrying a hijacked AER DOI (10.1257/aer.103.6.2121 = Autor & Dorn 2013)
      [102] venue "Journal of Technology Law and Practice" on "Fairness, AI & recruitment - ScienceDirect.com" (actual venue: Computer Law & Security Review)
Every one of those strings is a byte-substring of its own bib record. The validator PASSES all of them. The lever would ship "...in the American Economic Review..." for a blog post and "...in the Review of AI..." for a journal that does not exist. That is a FABRICATED ATTRIBUTION shipped by a validator that checks PROVENANCE-FROM-SIDECAR, not TRUTH — it launders garbage into authoritative prose. Brief 5 is unconditional: "No fabricated fact, number, quote or attribution may ever ship. Any design that can leak one is unshippable, whatever it scores."
Crossref cannot repair this. Only 26/105 have a DOI, and the failure mode is a broken DOI<->document binding: [82] carries a REAL AER DOI on a blog post, so Crossref will faithfully return "American Economic Review" and the validator will pass it. Crossref verifies that a DOI exists, not that the DOI belongs to the document we cited.

=== 4. INVERSION: the lever's own premise cuts against it ===
The premise is right — prose naming is the only source-quality channel that survives RACE. The corollary is that it is also the only channel through which a WEAK source base can reach the judge. Today the judge sees ZERO citations and CANNOT audit our sourcing; "Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles" scores on an unfalsifiable default (IF .4409, and bodhi proves .5559 is reachable with one venue name). Open the channel and the base becomes countable: after L5's journal-tier filter, exactly 9 records have BOTH a journal venue and a DOI. Sixty rotations of "X, in Y, found..." over ~6 distinct journals does not demonstrate "a broad and representative selection of current, high-quality academic literature" — it demonstrates a literature review built on six papers, evaluated by a criterion literally named "Depth and Representativeness." The downside on the exact criterion being targeted exceeds the (sub-noise) upside.

VERDICT: refuted. Upside is capped at +0.009 under maximally generous assumptions (below the noise floor), the board shows zero (slightly negative) association between attribution density and both target criteria, the mechanism's safety proof is demonstrably false against the actual sidecar, and the mechanism makes a thin evidence base visible on the criterion it is meant to raise.
input.fix:
Do not ship in-prose venue/author injection. Keep the deterministic sidecar-copy machinery but change WHAT is copied to the two channels the systems above us actually use, neither of which requires trustworthy venue metadata:

(1) ORPHAN-SUBJECT REPAIR WITHOUT ATTRIBUTION (class F, still byte-copied). Replace "The study" / "One analysis" / "That study" (11 occurrences in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank10_sections_compose/report.md) with a METHOD/DESIGN descriptor lifted from that citation's own verified span: "A firm-level panel study", "A randomized field experiment", "A task-exposure mapping", "An IV design using AI-graduate supply". This is bodhi's exact signal ("event-study designs using exogenous shocks", "(This evidence is correlational rather than a clean causal estimate)") and bodhi holds the board's best comprehensiveness AND best instruction-following. A method descriptor cannot be a false attribution — it names no journal.

(2) AGGREGATE LITERATURE CHARACTERIZATION as CLASS I typed moves. The 0.5102 reference signals source quality with "A strong consensus emerges from the literature regarding...", "A dominant theme in the literature is...", "remains contested, with different empirical studies yielding conflicting conclusions". These are entity-free, digit-free, premise-anchored relational rulings — already legal under the revised CLASS I contract, and they feed insight (w=0.32) as well as comprehensiveness, so they clear the noise floor where a 0.081-weight lever cannot.

(3) NAME ONLY THE 4-6 CANONICAL THEORY ANCHORS, not per-claim. The reference does exactly this ("prominently associated with economists like Daron Acemoglu and David Autor"; "Influential studies, such as the one by Frey and Osborne"). Acemoglu/Restrepo (task model), Autor, Frey/Osborne, Brynjolfsson — verifiable, canonical, cheap, and it is a framework-attribution, not a finding-attribution, so it cannot mint a false claim.

(4) PREREQUISITE IF ANYONE REVIVES VENUE ATTRIBUTION LATER: a byte-substring validator is not sufficient and never was. The venue field must be REBUILT, not backfilled — re-resolve each source URL through Crossref/OpenAlex FROM THE URL (not the stored DOI, which is hijacked in at least entry [82]), require the returned title to fuzzy-match the stored source_title above a threshold, and DROP every entry that fails. Until the DOI<->document binding is independently verified, no venue string in bibliography.json may enter prose.

CHEAP TEST that would have caught this before any compose: manually audit 5 injected attributions against Crossref. Entries [41], [46], [82], [100] and [102] fail today.
